t_ch: 是了，我也爱他，让他别来捣乱了。
t_en: Good. I love him too. Now tell him to stop causing trouble.

t_ch: 我投降，我投降！
t_en: I surrender! I surrender!

t_ch: 你干啥了你就投降。
t_en: What have you done? Why are you surrendering?

t_ch: 已经顾不得那么多了，你快步越至二人跟前，以迅雷不及掩耳之势扑向控制台。
t_en: There's no time for all this. You hurry your steps over toward the two operators and rush over to the control station.

t_ch: 哇——这么多插口，玉兆到底该插在哪儿？
t_en: Woah... There are so many ports. Which one is the jade abacus supposed to go into?

t_ch: 算了，随便选一个吧。
t_en: Forget it. Just stick it in a random one.

t_ch: 住手啊——！
t_en: Stop!

t_ch: 就像是弹奏一曲乐章，竞锋舰舰炮以既定的节律歌唱，这就是老白提到的「礼炮精神」。
t_en: The Skysplitter's cannons begin to ring out in a set rhythm like a roaring symphony. This is it! This is the "cannon spirit" that Old Bai mentioned.

t_ch: 在「演武仪典二十一响」的炮声里，你终于理解了——放炮一时爽，一直放炮一直爽。
t_en: In the booming of the "Wardance <unbreak>21</unbreak>-Gun Salute," you finally understand... One explosion can make you feel great for a moment, and constant explosions can make you feel great forever.

t_ch: 你一边走在舰船边缘，一边欣赏礼炮的轰鸣，不知不觉间，已然回到来处。
t_en: You walk along the border of the ship as you admire the booming salute. Before you know it, you find yourself back where you came from.

t_ch: 除开右舷第二组舰炮的炮声略显沉闷，其余都堪称完美。
t_en: The second group of cannons on the starboard side sounded a little dulled, but otherwise, that was perfection.

t_ch: 怎么样，{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}感受到了「礼炮精神」吗？
t_en: Well, {F#miss}{M#mister}, did you feel the "cannon spirit"?

t_ch: 才二十一响，真没玩够。
t_en: Twenty-one explosions just weren't enough.

t_ch: 这就是你不懂了，按照星际惯例，鸣放二十一响是最高礼遇。
t_en: That's where you're mistaken. According to interastral custom, the <unbreak>21</unbreak>-gun salute is considered the highest courtesy.

t_ch: 老早就想这么做了，好爽！
t_en: I've always wanted to do that, and that feels great!

t_ch: 你明白了吧，这就是我找上你的原因。
t_en: Now you understand why I came to you.

t_ch: 放炮一时爽，坐牢悔断肠。
t_en: Setting off a cannon may be fun, but you'll regret it in prison when you're done!

t_ch: 你还别说，他们看到程序之后就立刻来找我了，我大方承认就是我干的。
t_en: Enough of that. As soon as they saw the program, they came looking for me and I graciously admitted that it was my work.

t_ch: 绝不给你添麻烦！
t_en: I won't cause you any trouble!

t_ch: 仁科！你不也有事情要拜托{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}吗，你倒是说两句啊！
t_en: Renke! You have something you wished to ask of the {F#girl}{M#boy}, don't you? Say something!

t_ch: 快点说吧，得加钱。
t_en: Make it quick. I'm charging you separately.

t_ch: 我会用老白的钱包打白条的。
t_en: I'll give you an IOU from Old Bai.

t_ch: 怎么，还没结束吗？
t_en: What? Are we not done here?

t_ch: 就帮我个小忙。
t_en: It's just a small favor.

t_ch: 唉！年轻人，你注意过竞锋舰上面的表演方队吗？
t_en: Well! Youngster, have you ever noticed the Skysplitter display team?

t_ch: 注意到了。
t_en: I have.

t_ch: 但你没有注意到它们的编队飞行吧？
t_en: But you've never seen them fly in formation?

t_ch: 没注意，怎么了？
t_en: I haven't, why?

t_ch: 我就知道。
t_en: I knew it.

t_ch: 在退休前，我编队云骑阵列，在退休后，我受邀编队阵列。我设计了一套出神入化的飞行编队表演，给竞锋舰的演武仪典献礼！
t_en: Before I retired, I put together a Cloud Knight display team, and I have been invited to do the same since my retirement. I designed an unbelievable flight formation performance, especially for the Skysplitter Wardance.

t_ch: 「六机三角队俯冲」、「六机编队横滚」、「五机水平开花」、「四机垂直开花」…可你猜怎么着？如此精彩的表演，却根本没有人注意到！
t_en: "The six-plane triangle dive," "the six-plane side roll," "the five-plane horizontal chandelle," "the four-plane vertical chandelle..." But you know what? No one even noticed!

t_ch: 观众都往下看，向上看伤脖子。
t_en: The spectators were all looking down, as looking up hurts your neck.

t_ch: 有道理，但不足以说服我。
t_en: That may be true, but it's not enough to convince me!

t_ch: 这还能怪谁，彩蛋藏得太深了！
t_en: Well, who can you blame for that? Your talents are clearly too well hidden.

t_ch: 我曾经相信他们肯定能发现！
t_en: I used to be certain that they'd notice!

t_ch: 如果你站在演武仪典的擂台往上看，你能看到整整五分钟不重样的编队表演！
t_en: If you stand on the Wardance stage and look up, you can see a full five-minute flight display with no repetition!

t_ch: 偌大的竞锋舰，居然没有一个人懂飞行编队的魅力，这届观众到底行不行？
t_en: The Skysplitter is so vast, and you're telling me that not a single person here understands the beauty of a display team? Are you sure these spectators are even fit for the job?

t_ch: 仁科是这样的，一谈起飞行编队，道德水平就有些松懈。
t_en: Renke is just like this... As soon as he gets talking about his display team, his morality gets a little lax.

t_ch: 所以，我想让你做的事显而易见……
t_en: So, what I want you to do is obvious...

t_ch: 我好想看看飞行编队啊…
t_en: I really want to see the display team...

t_ch: 哈哈，我从见你第一面起，就知道你品味非凡了。
t_en: Haha! I knew that you had fine taste from the moment I met you!

t_ch: 不会是让我用炮把它打下来吧？
t_en: You're not asking me to shoot them all down with the cannons, are you?

t_ch: 年轻人，有品！
t_en: Fine taste, youngster!

t_ch: 你闭嘴！
t_en: Shut your trap!

t_ch: 嗨呀，谁没事看这个呀？
t_en: Sheesh, who would care about watching that?

t_ch: 哼哼，只有没亲眼看过飞行编队表演的人，才会做出如此无知的发言。
t_en: Humph! Only someone who had never seen my display team with their own eyes would dare make such an ignorant statement.

t_ch: 百闻不如一见，我现在就带你去见识见识，什么叫无敌的飞行表演。
t_en: Well, seeing is believing. I will take you to go and see a truly unbelievable flying performance for yourself.

t_ch: 说走咱就走。
t_en: Alright, let's go.

t_ch: 我先喘口气。
t_en: Let me catch my breath for a second.

t_ch: 百闻不如一见，我现在就带你去见识见识，什么叫无敌的飞行表演。
t_en: Well, seeing is believing. I will take you to go and see a truly unbelievable flying performance for yourself.

t_ch: 说走咱就走。
t_en: Alright, let's go.

t_ch: 我先喘口气。
t_en: Let me catch my breath for a second.

t_ch: 你们来到竞锋舰的擂台中，你感觉到仁科以某种方式固定住了你的视角。
t_en: As you arrive at the Skysplitter stage, you feel Renke fix your line of sight using some strange methods.

t_ch: 挺胸！抬头！再抬高一点，埋着头怎么能看清天上的飞行编队呢？
t_en: Chest out! Head up! Higher! How are you going to see my display team in the skies if your head is buried like that?

t_ch: 仁科以他八十万云骑教习的臂力按住你的脑袋，硬生生把你的视线固定在上方。
t_en: Renke uses the arm strength he used to train <unbreak>800,000</unbreak> Cloud Knights to forcibly fix your line of sight on the skies.

t_ch: 这套阵型以仙舟古兵法为基础改编，退可聚拢为守，进可直进为攻，变幻无穷。
t_en: This formation is adapted from ancient Xianzhou military formations. As they retreat, they tighten ranks to defend. As they attack, they advance straight. The routine changes endlessly.

t_ch: 可不要觉得它就只有这点能耐，大道至简，衍化至繁，正是以这样的简单方阵为基础，才能在大规模作战上，呈现出复杂的变化。
t_en: But don't think that this is all they've got to offer. From the most simple to the most complex maneuvers, they are all based on this simple square formation to allow for complex changes in large-scale combat operations.

t_ch: 似乎是被你们的古怪行为吸引，更多的观众抬头看向天空，相机的快门声开始层出不穷。
t_en: Seemingly lured in by your strange behavior, more spectators have begun to raise their eyes to the skies as the sounds of camera shutters begin to snap endlessly.

t_ch: 我看到了，差不多可以走了吗？
t_en: I've seen it. Can we go now?

t_ch: 这才哪到哪？
t_en: You've barely seen anything yet!

t_ch: 大哥，你轻点，你不是在抢劫。
t_en: Be gentler on people, that felt like a robbery.

t_ch: 你放心，我不要钱，我只要你抬头看。
t_en: Relax, I don't want your money. I just want you to raise your head.

t_ch: 当然，我也不会一直按着你的头，要求你抬头看编队表演…现在你可以自由活动，自由观察，要是看完有任何心得，请记得向我报告！
t_en: Of course, I'm not planning on holding your head the whole time and forcing you to keep watching the flight team... You are free now, free to watch as you please. If you have any thoughts to share after watching, make sure to come and tell me!

t_ch: 别急！最精彩的部分还没来——
t_en: Hold on! The best part is still to come —

t_ch: 竟然还没有结束吗？
t_en: It's still not over yet?

t_ch: 编队我都能背下了。
t_en: I've memorized all the formations already.

t_ch: 我列车煤气忘关了。
t_en: I forgot to turn off the gas on the Express...

t_ch: ——等等，你看，他们终于注意到了，我那精妙的编队！
t_en: ...Wait, look! They've finally noticed, my exquisite flight squad!

t_ch: 据说，你被仁科按着脑袋讲解阵法的视频，迅速窜上了各大门户网站的热搜榜。
t_en: Rumor has it that Renke holding your head while explaining the correct view to watch the display quickly became a trending topic on various popular sites.

t_ch: 就这样，仁科终于实现了愿望，真是可喜可贺，可喜可贺。
t_en: Just like that, Renke was able to fulfill his dream. Truly a joy, truly a reason to celebrate.

t_ch: 至此，我们俩竞锋舰老东西终于是满足愿望了。
t_en: Just like that, we two old Skysplitter veterans have fulfilled our long-held dreams.

t_ch: 谢谢你呀，{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}。
t_en: Thank you, {F#miss}{M#mister}.

t_ch: 确实能感受到你们的爱。
t_en: I can really feel your love for it.

t_ch: 我对竞锋舰的爱，不输给这里的任何一个人。
t_en: No one has a greater love for the Skysplitter than I do.

t_ch: 咱也是。
t_en: Nor me.

t_ch: 应该是没别的心愿了吧？
t_en: That's it for your wishes, surely?

t_ch: 还想再放几炮。
t_en: I want to set off the cannons again.

t_ch: 你就别再难为{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}了。
t_en: Stop trying to make it difficult for {F#her}{M#him}.

t_ch: 我现在有一种养大的闺女嫁人的感觉，又寂寞又开心，哎……
t_en: It feels comparable to if my daughter has grown up, and I'm finally giving her away on her wedding day. It's a bittersweet happiness...

t_ch: 咱也将毕生所学留在了这里，希望这里的观众抬起头时，能想起她曾经的使命。
t_en: We also gave this place all of our life-long knowledge. I hope that when the spectators raise their heads to the skies, they will remember the great mission she once carried.

t_ch: 最后，再次感谢你，实现了我俩的心愿。
t_en: Finally, thank you again for helping to fulfill our wishes.

t_ch: 这是你应得的报酬，再见！
t_en: These are your much deserved rewards. Farewell!

t_ch: {F#她}{M#他}要来了，你准备好…
t_en: {F#She's}{M#He's} coming. Get ready...

t_ch: 这事行不行啊…
t_en: Are you sure about this...?

t_ch: （这里应该就是老白提到的控制室了吧。）
t_en: (This must be the control room that Old Bai mentioned.)

t_ch: （看看这附近有没有适合插入玉兆的地方好了……）
t_en: (I just need to look around for a place where the jade abacus can fit...)

t_ch: 你听我接着讲——这套阵法的一个精髓就在于，它看似有所重复，却始终保持变化，让敌人捉摸不透。攻头则尾至，攻尾则首至，攻中则首尾俱至。
t_en: Let me explain further... The true beauty of this matrix lies in that it seems to repeat itself, but is constantly undergoing changes, preventing the enemy from predicting what lies ahead. Attack the head and the tail will lash out, attack the tail and the head will strike, attack the middle and both ends will convene on you.

t_ch: 战场之上，那股勃勃生机、万物竞发的境界，犹在眼前。
t_en: The thriving vitality and fierce competition of the battlefield are shown right here, before your eyes.

t_ch: 我…好像什么也看不见呢？
t_en: I... I don't see anything?

t_ch: 怎么可能，你再仔细看看。哦，我明白了，你肯定是用手机在看，对吧？
t_en: How? Take a closer look! Oh, I get it. You've been watching on your phone, haven't you?

t_ch: 怎么？我一直都是用手机。
t_en: So? I watch everything on my phone.

t_ch: 这就是问题的症结所在啊！
t_en: That's where the problem is!

t_ch: 这跟手机又有什么关系呢？
t_en: What's this got to do with my phone?

t_ch: 那可太有关系了！
t_en: It has everything to do with it!

t_ch: 你必须得去换一台更加强劲的设备来看飞行编队！懂了吗？手机看不见！
t_en: You need a far more powerful device to properly enjoy a flight demonstration! Do you understand me? You'll never see it on a phone!

t_ch: 这世界上有很多「不留心，看不见」的东西，我的飞行编队也是这样的。
t_en: There are many things in this world that just will not be seen if they are not observed closely, and my flight team is one of those things.

t_ch: …不过，你不必担心，就算没有我陪着你，你自己来也能看到编队表演。记得换一台更好的设备啊！
t_en: ...However, there is no need to worry. You will still be able to see my flight team's performance, even if I'm not with you. Make sure you watch it on something better than a phone, though!

t_ch: 飞行编队迟迟未到，你感觉身后的仁科隐隐有一些不安感。
t_en: The flight team is still yet to arrive. Behind you, you can sense a restlessness emanating from Renke.

t_ch: 就像是给别人吹了半天牛「某某电影天下第一」，结果到电影院后才发现它没有上院线排期——你想向世人证明的全部艺术与巧思，竟是如此的一文不值。
t_en: It's like you excitedly tell your friends about a movie being "the best thing you'll ever see, a work of art" only to get to the cinema and find out it's not even due to be released in theaters... All the artistic talent and ingenuity that you wanted to prove to the world has gone up in smoke, just like that...

t_ch: 你本可以转过头去给仁科说一声「算了吧，下次再来看」，但事情此时出现了转机……
t_en: Just as you planned to turn back to Renke and tell him, "Let's forget it for today. We'll try again next time..." there was a sudden turn around...

t_ch: 仁科引以为傲的飞行编队，它来啦！
t_en: Renke's pride and joy, the flight team, it's here!

t_ch: 一位云骑军在这扇门的旁边，既不像守门，又不像要进入。在他进退维谷之时看到了你，隔着头盔露出遇到了救星的目光。
t_en: Standing by the door, you see a Cloud Knight neither guarding it nor seeming to want to go inside. Just when he seems lost in his dilemma, he catches sight of you, and an attitude of seeing his savior pours forth through the gaps in his helmet.

t_ch: 这位{F#姑娘}{M#兄弟}请留步！我知道阁下身手不错，请快来帮我个忙！
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, please wait! I know you possess considerable talent. Please help me!

t_ch: 眼下有个危险的犯人劫持了一名七百多岁的…老者。奇怪，仙舟上向来也不会强调七百岁能有多老来着……
t_en: There is a dangerous criminal that has taken a seven-hundred-year old... old man hostage. Strange, would anyone on the Xianzhou really stress that a seven-hundred-year old was an old man...?

t_ch: 我需要你协助我守住这扇门。目前犯人还没被惊动，我去搬些救兵。
t_en: I need you to help me guard this door. The criminal is not aware of us yet. I need to go and get reinforcements.

t_ch: 何须救兵？一根球棒足矣！
t_en: Who needs reinforcement? One baseball bat is enough for me!

t_ch: 虽然阁下的威名我也有所耳闻，但云骑军还是不能以人质的安危去冒险。尤其被劫持的又是位老者。
t_en: Although I have heard tell of your great strength, as a Cloud Knight, I cannot take risks when it comes to the safety of a hostage. That is especially true when the hostage is an elderly man.

t_ch: 太看得起我了！我哪拦得住！
t_en: I think you overestimate me! How am I supposed to stop them?

t_ch: 阁下多虑了，只要别打草惊蛇，那劫匪不会贸然走出这扇门。更何况人质又是一名行动不便的老者。
t_en: Rest assured, the kidnapper won't come rushing out of this door as long as we don't go alert them. Plus, their hostage is an old man with limited mobility.

t_ch: 你就不能…呃…打个电话？
t_en: Can you not just... uhh... make a phone call?

t_ch: 让阁下见笑了，这鬼地方的信号实在是不好…等这件事结束，我一定会参那个黑心承建商一本。
t_en: Though I'm ashamed to admit it, the signal in this place is no good... When this is all over, I will go and file a complaint against that no-good contractor.

t_ch: 总之还请您务必守住这扇门，如果劫匪想要破门转移，也请帮我拖延一下时间。我去去就回！
t_en: Anyway, please stay here and keep a close eye on this door. If the kidnapper tries to break the door down or make a move, please try to buy us some time. I'll be right back!

t_ch: 还未等你同意或拒绝，那位云骑便从你的视线中消失了。现在整个世界只留下你一个人去应对这扇门。
t_en: The Cloud Knight disappeared in front of your very eyes before you even had time to say no. Now, you are the only person left to face this mysterious door.

t_ch: 嗯…稍等一下！那位云骑士兵会不会是在耍你？先听听门后的情况吧。
t_en: Hmm... Wait a second! How do you know that this Cloud Knight isn't just messing you around? Maybe try listening in and seeing what's going on.

t_ch: 老头，刚才不是很嚣张么？
t_en: You had a lot more to say for yourself before, old man. Why so quiet now?

t_ch: 哼哼，年轻人别太得意。老夫虽然失手被你擒住，但老夫膝下还有七个孙子，他们各个身怀绝技。万一半天不见老夫，一定会过来寻找。
t_en: Humph, don't be so smug, youngster. I may have been careless and let you capture me, but I have seven grandsons, each of them with their own unique skills. If I go missing for a while, they're sure to come looking!

t_ch: 而且老夫还突然感应到，门外有位高手。
t_en: Plus, I can sense that there is a powerful someone behind that door right now.

t_ch: 这都被你发现了。
t_en: You noticed me.

t_ch: 毕竟老夫可不是什么寻常人，哼哼。
t_en: Well, I am no normal old man, hehe.

t_ch: 什么？还有高手？
t_en: What? There's someone strong here?

t_ch: 门外的高手，请你自信一点。
t_en: A little more confidence, please, master!

t_ch: 既然如此，那我进来！
t_en: If that's the case, I'm coming in!

t_ch: 门外的的高手，请你冷静一点。
t_en: Master, please stay calm.

t_ch: 不过不劳烦您出手，老夫那几位孙子都是来参加演武仪典的种子选手，还望您给年轻人一些表现自我的机会。
t_en: There is no need for you to get involved in this. My grandsons are all seed competitors that came to take part in the Wardance. Please give these youngsters a chance to prove themselves.

t_ch: 别胡说八道了！你…你不过是虚张声势罢了！
t_en: Enough nonsense! Y—You're just blowing hot air!

t_ch: 「老夫膝下还有七个孙子」似乎言犹在耳，你回过神来，发现身后突然站了好多人。
t_en: As the words "I have seven grandsons" seem to echo through your ears, you suddenly notice a huge group of men behind you.

t_ch: 爷爷的行踪到这里就不见了，估计人还在附近。
t_en: Grandpa's trail ends here, so he must still be nearby.

t_ch: 请问，你有见过一位老者么？
t_en: Excuse me, have you seen an old man?

t_ch: 你们要找的人就在门后。
t_en: He's behind this door.

t_ch: 果然！找到了！爷爷在这里！
t_en: I knew it! We found him! Grandpa's here!

t_ch: 没见过，不知道你们在说什么。
t_en: No, I don't know what you're talking about?

t_ch: 嗯？这个味道，是谎言的味道！
t_en: Hm? That scent, it's the scent of deceit and lies!

t_ch: 你们从哪里冒出来的？
t_en: Where did you all come from?

t_ch: 从来处来。
t_en: We come from whence we came.

t_ch: 到去处去。
t_en: And go to where we will go.

t_ch: 好了你们不要故弄玄虚了，快来救我！
t_en: That's enough pointless riddles! Come and save me!

t_ch: 门外刚来的那六个人，我数数…你们尽量站得让我能看清，哦，那就是你的七个孙子？
t_en: Those six outside that just arrived, let me count them... Stand still so I can count you properly! Oh, so these are your seven grandsons?

t_ch: 正是如此。你有难了。
t_en: That's right. You're in for it now.

t_ch: 一、二、三、四、五…怎么才六个？难道那个灰毛也是？
t_en: One, two, three, four, five... Why are there only six? Is that one with the gray hair yours as well?

t_ch: 那个我不认识…但是，我知道{F#她}{M#他}是个高手！现在老夫不方便脱身，就请你来组织我的孙子们进行营救吧。
t_en: No, I don't know {F#her}{M#him}... But, I know {F#she}{M#he} is a powerful martial master! I can't escape right now, so I need you to guide my grandsons and orchestrate my rescue!

t_ch: 孙子们，你们要服从这个灰发高手的命令！
t_en: My grandsons, you must heed the orders of this gray-haired master!

t_ch: 好的，爷爷！请尽情吩咐我们吧，灰毛{F#姑娘}{M#兄弟}！
t_en: Yes, grandpa! Please give us your orders, gray-haired {F#lady}{M#sir}!

t_ch: 灰毛{F#姑娘}{M#兄弟}，我们该让谁先出战呢？
t_en: Gray-haired {F#lady}{M#sir}, who should go into battle first?

t_ch: （怎么突然让我来指挥…好吧，该让谁先上呢？）
t_en: (Why am I suddenly the commander...? Fine, who should I send out first?)

t_ch: 哼，没想到会让我出战…可怜的劫匪，你要遭重了！我力能单手托起星槎，身形可变大变小。区区劫匪，不过蝼蚁！
t_en: Humph, I did not expect you to send me into the contest... Foolish kidnapper, you're in for it now! I can lift a starskiff with a single hand and make myself bigger or smaller as I please. I'll crush you like an ant, kidnapper!

t_ch: 很好，很有精神！
t_en: There we go. That's the spirit!

t_ch: 要不我和你一起上？
t_en: You want me to go with you?

t_ch: 不必，我一人足矣。
t_en: There is no need. I can handle this alone.

t_ch: 你不带个兄弟吗？
t_en: Will you not take a brother with you?

t_ch: 不必，我一人足矣。
t_en: There is no need. I can handle this alone.

t_ch: 我早已受够了繁文缛节！
t_en: Enough rambling!

t_ch: 红衣武者脱去上衣，露出虬结的肌肉，独自冲进了房间。
t_en: The red-clad warrior takes off his top to expose his buff, wiry upper body and charges into the room alone.

t_ch: 忽然门外听到砰的一声巨响，像是什么巨大体型的生物一头撞向了天花板，然后栽倒在地。
t_en: Suddenly, you hear a huge thud from outside the door, as if a giant creature has hit its head on the ceiling and collapsed.

t_ch: 没想到你个小毛贼还有点手段。
t_en: I'll give it to you, leech. You have some party tricks.

t_ch: 喂，不关我的事啊，他自己忽然变大撞到了头，怎么怪我！
t_en: Hey, that has nothing to do with me! He suddenly enlarged himself and knocked his own head. How can you blame me for that?

t_ch: 听声音是个好头。
t_en: Sounds like we're off to a head-start.

t_ch: 原来是打头阵啊？
t_en: So he's more of a headbanger?

t_ch: 早已预感到结局。
t_en: I saw that coming...

t_ch: 无妨！老夫养孙千日、用孙一时，灰毛高手，让我的下个孙子出手吧！
t_en: No need to fear! I have raised these grandsons for years for this very moment. Gray-haired master, send in my next grandson!

t_ch: 哼，我向来不喜暴力，对付贼人要用智取。只需让我看一眼便知贼人的破绽。招数动向，尽在掌握。
t_en: Humph, I have never been a fan of violence. Defeating a kidnapper is all about using your wits. Give me one look at this villain and I shall know his weaknesses, his movements, and his trends.

t_ch: 听上去都不是攻击。
t_en: None of those sounds like attack.

t_ch: 要不我和你一起上？
t_en: You want me to go with you?

t_ch: 任何招数都躲不开我的法眼，你不要小看我！
t_en: No technique can escape my discerning eyes. Do not underestimate me!

t_ch: 你不带个兄弟吗？
t_en: Will you not take a brother with you?

t_ch: 任何招数都躲不开我的法眼，你不要小看我！
t_en: No technique can escape my discerning eyes. Do not underestimate me!

t_ch: 相信我，会赢的！
t_en: Believe me, I shall have victory!

t_ch: 橙衣武者带着洞悉一切的自信，从容地走入门中。
t_en: The orange-clad warrior calmly walked through the door, confident that he could perceive all.

t_ch: 放弃吧，羸弱的劫匪。你的一切招数都会被我看破。我眼观六路，看穿隐身法；耳听八方，能够收集远处的声波！
t_en: Give up now, pathetic kidnapper! I can see through any technique you may have! My eyes see in all directions and expose all stealth techniques. My ears hear all sound waves, no matter the distance!

t_ch: 你在说什么……
t_en: What are you talking about...

t_ch: 一切花招都逃不过我的耳目。言语会骗人，但肌肉和心跳不会。
t_en: No flashy technique can deceive my eyes and ears. Though you can lie with your words, your muscles and heart rate tell the truth.

t_ch: 就好像你，下盘微微挪步，右手握拳蓄力。心里想的一定想趁我说话时贴近我，冲我鼻子…哎哟！
t_en: Just like how your lower body has moved slightly, and your fist is clenching tight. I am certain that you are planning to approach me while I talk and punch me in the nose... Oof!

t_ch: 从声音上判断，这是一记干净利落的打击。
t_en: Judging from the sound, that was a one-hit KO...

t_ch: 呃…还别说，你说的倒是准……
t_en: Ahh... look at that, you were right...

t_ch: 孙子！！！
t_en: Grandson!!!

t_ch: 抱歉，我实在没忍住。
t_en: Sorry, I could not help myself.

t_ch: 哼哼，莫要嚣张！灰毛高手！放我的下一个孙子进来！
t_en: Humph, don't get so cocky! Gray-haired master! Send in my next grandson!

t_ch: 爷爷，别急，我这就来救你！真正的高手能让对方任何攻击都无效！
t_en: Grandpa, don't worry, I will save you! A true master will make all of their enemy's attacks completely useless!

t_ch: 听上去好像很有道理。
t_en: That actually sounds like it makes sense...

t_ch: 要不我和你一起上？
t_en: You want me to go with you?

t_ch: 这怎么行？那可是我的爷爷！我一定要亲自营救他！
t_en: How can I allow this? That is my grandfather! I must save him myself!

t_ch: 你不带个兄弟吗？
t_en: Will you not take a brother with you?

t_ch: 我可是爷爷最喜欢的孙子！你们都别去，我一定要亲自营救他！
t_en: I am his favorite grandson! You all stay back, and I will save him myself!

t_ch: 想我未学走路，先学内功。任何招数都伤不到我分毫，从小到大我就不知道伤口是什么意思。爷爷稍等片刻，孙子这就来救你！
t_en: Before I learned to walk, I learned to channel my inner energy! No attack can harm me, and I have never in my life known the meaning of a wound. Wait for me, grandfather! I'm coming to save you!

t_ch: 劫匪老兄，你知道吗？只有坚韧的躯体才是不败法门。
t_en: Let me teach you one thing, kidnapper. Only a body that perseveres can be undefeated.

t_ch: 他…他在发光…这琥珀色的光芒…难道！
t_en: He... he's glowing...! That amber glow... could it be!?

t_ch: 没错，我这孙子修习的正是「补天神功」。一旦发动，恰如补天司命降世临凡，谁也无法伤他分毫。
t_en: That's right. This grandson of mine practices the technique of Skymending. As soon as it is activated, he is like the Firmamend Arbiter among mortals. No man can harm him!

t_ch: 那那那…那你要用什么招数来对付我？呵，别嚣张，我可不会怕你这装神弄鬼的小技巧！
t_en: S—s—so... how are you going to overpower me? Hah, don't be so arrogant! I'm not scared of some little supernatural magic tricks!

t_ch: 对付你？不。我修习的「补天神功」并不能用来战斗，只能用来防御。只要我不破功，就没人能伤我分毫。
t_en: Overpower you? No. I have mastered Skymending, but it cannot be used for offense, only defense. As long as I do not disrupt my technique, no one can harm me.

t_ch: …那你也没法伤我分毫？
t_en: ...So, you can't harm me either?

t_ch: 正是如此。
t_en: Indeed.

t_ch: 那我不管你了，你自己在这里罚站吧。
t_en: Then why should I even bother with you? Go stand in the corner.

t_ch: 老头，叫你的下一个孙子进来吧！
t_en: Old man, call in your next grandson!

t_ch: …下个孙子！！！快来救你爷爷！！！
t_en: ...Next grandson!!! Come and save grandpa!!!

t_ch: 这劫匪太狡猾了！看来只有我们兄弟同心才能解决。
t_en: This kidnapper is tricksy! It looks like we will have to use our sibling bond to deal with him!

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: Exactly.

t_ch: 预祝你们成功吧……
t_en: I hope this one works for you...

t_ch: 期待你们热血沸腾的组合技！
t_en: Show us a hot-blooded combo attack!

t_ch: 要不我和你们一起上？
t_en: Do you want me to attack with you?

t_ch: 抱歉，我兄弟二人合作无间，容不下第三者插手。
t_en: Sorry, our combination is seamless, and there is no room for a third to interfere.

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: Exactly.

t_ch: 你们不多带点兄弟吗？
t_en: Why don't you take a few more brothers?

t_ch: 抱歉，我兄弟二人合作无间，容不下第三者插手。
t_en: Sorry, our combination is seamless, and there is no room for a third to interfere.

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: Exactly.

t_ch: 我与五弟配合，一个能驾驭天雷，一个可以操控流水。想不到这贼人竟能使我两兄弟联手，这真是他的荣幸！
t_en: Brother Five and I have the perfect combination. One of us controls water and the other conducts lightning. I cannot believe this kidnapper has forced us to join forces. This is truly an honor for him!

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: Exactly.

t_ch: 你流水别把爷爷也给冲走…
t_en: Don't flush your grandpa away...

t_ch: 你天雷别把爷爷霹成老熟人…
t_en: Don't shock your grandpa into smithereens...

t_ch: 莫要担心，我二人在「巡猎」的命途上钻研颇深，擅长对单作战，不会殃及无辜。
t_en: No need to fear. We have dedicated great study to the path of The Hunt. We excel at solo contest, and we will not harm the innocent.

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: Exactly.

t_ch: 那你们虚构叙事有难咯。
t_en: Then you might have some problems with Pure Fiction.

t_ch: 随便吧，我累了。
t_en: Whatever, I'm tired.

t_ch: 总之看我兄弟两人强强联手，救回爷爷吧！
t_en: Just sit back and watch our powerful combination as we save our grandpa!

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: Exactly.

t_ch: 你不免为门后的劫匪感到担心，从两人身上的斗气中，仿佛能感受到惊雷与海啸的怒意。
t_en: You cannot help but feel concern for the kidnapper behind the door. As you sense the fighting spirit of the two siblings, you feel as if you can sense the rage of pure lightning and roaring waves.

t_ch: 要不我们商量一下……
t_en: Why don't we talk this out...

t_ch: 住口，你劫持我家爷爷，早就失去交涉的权利了！
t_en: Silence. You kidnapped our grandfather, and you lost the right to negotiate a long time ago!

t_ch: 没错！好兄弟，我们得喊点什么壮壮胆气——
t_en: That's right! Brother, let us do a battle cry to evoke our courage...

t_ch: 友情啊——
t_en: For friendship!

t_ch: 羁绊啊——
t_en: For our bonds!

t_ch: 未来啊——
t_en: For the future!

t_ch: 别喊这些没有用的了，要喊招式！
t_en: Stop yelling nonsense and call out an attack!

t_ch: 煌煌威灵！
t_en: Brilliant Spirit!

t_ch: 苍龙濯世！
t_en: Dragon Flood!

t_ch: 别！
t_en: NO, DON'T!!!

t_ch: 刹那间，雷鸣闪动。竞锋舰上的灯集体频闪了几下。不多一会儿，光照又恢复正常。
t_en: At that moment, lightning strikes and thunder claps. The lights aboard the Skysplitter flickered in unison for a few moments before returning to normal.

t_ch: 那个…外面还有人么，他俩在地上抽搐，要不要叫个医生？
t_en: Um... Is there anyone else still out there? They're on the ground, twitching. Do you want to call a doctor or something?

t_ch: 别担心，这不是第一次了，他们已经习惯了……
t_en: Don't worry. This isn't the first time. They're used to it...

t_ch: 我第一次知道，练武术还讲究元素反应……
t_en: I never really thought that you had to consider elemental reactions when practicing martial arts...

t_ch: 唉…灰毛高手，请让我的下一位孙子进来吧！
t_en: Ah... Gray-haired master, please send in my next grandson!

t_ch: 终于到我们出场的时候了。
t_en: It's finally time for us to take the stage!

t_ch: 你们？你和谁？
t_en: Us? You and who?

t_ch: 这不就你一个人吗？
t_en: You're one person, aren't you?

t_ch: 看不起谁呢？
t_en: Are you looking down on me?

t_ch: 在紫色武者身旁的虚空中，突然传来一个略显不满的声音。
t_en: Suddenly, a slightly displeased voice starts to call out from the void behind the purple-clad warrior.

t_ch: 六哥，{F#她}{M#他}不是看不起你，只是看不见你。
t_en: Brother Six, {F#she}{M#he} isn't looking down on you. {F#She}{M#He} can't even see you.

t_ch: 哦哦，误会了，抱歉哈。我所练的是隐匿身形的本事，忘记寻常人看不见我了。
t_en: Ahh, I'm sorry. I misunderstood. I practice techniques to conceal myself, and I forget normal people cannot see me.

t_ch: 我虽然没什么武功，但有一个法宝紫金瓠。若我叫一句敌人的名字，只要他答应了，便会被吸入这紫金瓠中！
t_en: I may not have any martial arts skills, but I have a sacred vessel: my amethyst gold gourd! If I call my enemies' names, and they respond to me, they will be sucked inside the amethyst gold gourd!

t_ch: 听起来有点高魔…
t_en: This is some high-fantasy stuff...

t_ch: 画风怎么开始玄幻了？
t_en: When did this take a turn for the mystical?

t_ch: 六哥，咱们上吧。
t_en: Brother Six, let's go.

t_ch: 好叻！
t_en: Alright!

t_ch: 你似乎听到某种细微的声响，像是某种东西被吸入时发出的气流声。
t_en: You feel like you can hear a faint sound, like the sound of air being sucked inside something.

t_ch: *仙舟粗口*，完犊子了…啊！
t_en: *Xianzhou profanity*, this is bad... Agh!

t_ch: 还没完呢，六哥，咱们的高光时刻才刚刚开始！
t_en: It's not over yet, Brother Six. Our great moment to shine is just beginning!

t_ch: 只有一个人么？快点吧，我累了。
t_en: There's just one of you? Hurry up then, I'm getting tired.

t_ch: 嚯？这么嚣张。这次非要给你点厉害瞧瞧。六哥，动手吧！
t_en: Oh? So confident. Now you'll see some real power! Brother Six, now!

t_ch: 六哥？你个老六，人哪去了？别藏了！我刚才叫你名字的时候，你不还答应来着吗？
t_en: Brother Six? Where's he gone? Stop hiding! You responded when I called your name just now, so...

t_ch: …不对。
t_en: ...Wait...

t_ch: *仙舟粗口*，完犊子了…啊！
t_en: *Xianzhou profanity*, this is bad... Agh!

t_ch: 你听到了一拳将人打倒的声音，大概猜到了毫不意外的结局。
t_en: You hear the sound of a punch knocking someone to the ground and have a rough understanding of the unsurprising outcome.

t_ch: …说了多少次！不要随时开着你那个破葫芦！
t_en: ...How many times have I told you? Don't just leave that damn gourd open all the time!

t_ch: …你也别太着急，年纪这么大了，气坏了身体可不好。
t_en: ...Don't get so worked up, old man. Anger can be bad for your health at your age.

t_ch: 算了算了…孙子？孙子何在？快点再来个孙子！莫要辱没了我家的门风！
t_en: Forget it... Grandson? Where are you, grandson? I need another grandson! I cannot allow our family to be disgraced like this!

t_ch: 这就…结束了？
t_en: So... it's all over?

t_ch: 你已经没有孙子了。
t_en: You have no grandsons left!

t_ch: 这里只剩下我了。
t_en: I'm the only one here.

t_ch: 那…那就靠你了，灰毛高手！事成之后，我收你为我的义孙！
t_en: Then... Then I'm counting on you, gray-haired master! Complete this mission and I shall take you as my adopted grandson!

t_ch: 老东西，你占我便宜我就揍你！
t_en: Old man, take advantage of me like that, and I'll beat you myself!

t_ch: 我当你爷爷还差不多！
t_en: Why don't you just call me uncle?

t_ch: 还是给点更实际的奖励吧…
t_en: How about a more concrete reward...

t_ch: 好的，爷爷！我来救你了！
t_en: Okay, grandpa! I'm coming to save you!

t_ch: 这些事之后再讨论吧！先进来救救我吧！
t_en: We can discuss all this later! Come and save me first!

t_ch: 药王爷在上，这都什么乱七八糟的……
t_en: Great Lord of Sanctus Medicus, I can't handle this mess...

t_ch: 正当你准备掏出球棍冲进去时，劫匪和被绑架的老者走了出来，而增援的云骑军也到了。
t_en: Just as you prepare to draw your bat and enter the room, the kidnapper and the tied hostage walk out of the room, just in time to be greeted by the Cloud Knight reinforcements.

t_ch: 辛苦了！我叫了增援的部队过来援救人质…嗯？现在什么情况？
t_en: Thanks for your help! I called reinforcements to come and help save the hostage... Hm? What's going on?

t_ch: 我投降！我投降了！
t_en: I surrender! I surrender!

t_ch: 真不愧是你啊，{NICKNAME}！阁下太厉害了，竟能在短时间内完成这样教科书般的人质救援！
t_en: I should have expected no less from you, {NICKNAME}! To pull off a textbook hostage rescue in such a short amount of time is truly amazing!

t_ch: 别提了，伤亡惨重啊。
t_en: You have no idea. We suffered heavy losses.

t_ch: …人都丢尽了！
t_en: ...It's a TPK!

t_ch: 我感化了劫匪。
t_en: I have reformed the kidnapper.

t_ch: …求求你们，放过我吧。
t_en: ...Please, let me go.

t_ch: 军爷！快把我抓起来吧！我投降，我自首！这老头找了一帮子精神病，用车轮战来轮番折磨我啊！
t_en: Sir! Please, take me away! I surrender, I confess! This old man sent an army of crazy people to come and torture me one after another!

t_ch: 不，是老夫教学无方…老夫回去要给他们制定一套新的课程，让他们七位一体，团队作战，到时候再来找你试试招。
t_en: No, it was my teaching that failed me... I will return and give them a whole new education. I will teach them to fight as one. When that time comes, I will find you and test out their strength!

t_ch: 别别！老人家，我知错了，你放过我吧…把您家那七块活宝看好了，别再让他们出来折磨人了！求求你了！
t_en: No, please! Old man, I was wrong. Please let me go... Take your band of clowns home and keep an eye on them. Don't let them torture anyone ever again. I'm begging you!

t_ch: 呃…那既然罪犯已经伏法，剩下的事就交给我们吧。
t_en: Uhh... Well, as the criminal has been brought to justice, you can leave the rest to us.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，多亏了阁下的协助，我们才能毫发无损地解救出人质。非常感谢！
t_en: It is thanks to your help, {NICKNAME}, that we were able to rescue the hostage without him coming to harm. I am forever grateful!

t_ch: 受之有愧啊！我真的没出力！
t_en: It honestly has nothing to do with me! I didn't do anything!

t_ch: 坦白说我很能共情那位劫匪。
t_en: If I'm honest, I sympathize with the kidnapper.

t_ch: 区区小贼，何足道哉。
t_en: One simple kidnapper is no trouble for me.

t_ch: 哈哈，不管怎么说，这点奖励算是我们的心意，还请您收下吧。我先带嫌犯和相关证人回去了。有缘再见。
t_en: Haha, be that as it may, please accept this reward as a token of our gratitude. I'll be taking the criminal and witnesses back with me. May we meet again.

t_ch: 这可如何是好呀？
t_en: What am I supposed to do with all this?

t_ch: 这位同学，你的相关信息并未出现在本节课程的出席名单中，请仔细核对课表，避免走错教室。
t_en: Hello, your related information isn't on the attendance list of this lecture. Please review your lesson plan and avoid the wrong classroom.

t_ch: 我是来检查教学质量的。
t_en: I'm here to evaluate the teaching quality.

t_ch: 就不允许我勤奋好学吗？
t_en: Am I not allowed to be hardworking?

t_ch: …抱歉，已检索到身份为来自星穹列车的旁听生。
t_en: ...Apologies, we have verified your status as a guest student from the Astral Express.

t_ch: 你好，{NICKNAME}同学，这间教室本日的课程安排为：财富学院的「经济学基本原理」、筑梦学院的「建筑素描基础」、谐乐学院的「作曲理论入门」。
t_en: Hello, {NICKNAME}. Today's lesson schedule for this classroom is as such: Fortune Academy's "Basic Financial Principles," Dreamweaver Academy's "Architectural Sketching Basics," and Charmony Academy's "Composition Theory for Beginners."

t_ch: 请确认——以上是否为你想要旁听的内容。
t_en: Please confirm if the above are the lessons you wish to audit.

t_ch: 没错，我就想听这些！
t_en: That's right. I want to attend these lessons!

t_ch: 不好意思，我走错了。
t_en: Sorry, I have the wrong room.

t_ch: 随着铃声响起，你的心绪如海潮般起伏不定，但你已经准备好迎接今日的课程了——
t_en: As the bell rings, your emotions waver like the tide, but you have already decided to take on today's lessons...

t_ch: 你专注地望向讲台，如饥似渴地聆听着，仿佛沙漠中偶遇绿洲的旅人，不肯放过每一滴知识的甘霖。
t_en: You focus your gaze on the lectern, listening intently like a voracious learner, as if you are a traveler who stumbles upon an oasis in the desert, greedily gulping every drop of knowledge.

t_ch: 但很可惜，不管你如何胸怀壮志，脑容量终究是有极限的——
t_en: A pity though, no matter how tenacious your will is, there are limits to your mind...

t_ch: 你搞不懂那些图形的具体含义，它们只让你想起托帕，然后是账账，再然后是大大小小的宝箱…等你终于神游匹诺康尼一周之后，课程已然过去了大半。
t_en: You are confused by the exact meanings of the drawings — they only make you think of Topaz, and then Numby, and then a myriad of big and small chests... When you're done daydreaming all over Penacony, the lesson has passed its halfway mark.

t_ch: 「我是谁、我在哪、今天吃什么」——正当你迷茫的小脑袋瓜开始不自觉思考起人生的终极难题时，却见讲师突然转身，双手重重拍在讲台上。
t_en: "Who am I, where am I, what should I eat today" — just as your baffled little brain starts to ruminate on the ultimate questions of life, the lecturer suddenly spins around, both his hands slamming down hard on the lectern.

t_ch: 嘶——要来了！
t_en: Ssss... It's coming!

t_ch: 你不由惊恐万分：你被发现了？难道真的有智械能读取别人的思维！
t_en: You are utterly taken aback: Were you noticed? Do Intellitrons really possess the power to read thoughts!?

t_ch: 现在，开始点名——威廉·威尔逊！
t_en: Now for roll call — William Wilson!

t_ch: 到！
t_en: Here!

t_ch: 丽姬娅！
t_en: Ligeia!

t_ch: 这里~
t_en: Hereeee~

t_ch: 罗德里克·厄舍！
t_en: Roderick Usher!
I'm here...

t_ch: 你目瞪口呆地看着灵活的皮皮西如同土拨鼠那般，从一个个空位上冒出头，用迥然相异的嗓音和语气熟练地签着到。
t_en: Flabbergasted, you stare at the lively Pepeshi. As if he is a groundhog, he's popping his head up from various empty seats, skillfully answering the roll call in completely different tones and voices.

t_ch: 周围的学生似乎对此司空见惯，而点名的讲师头也不抬。
t_en: The surrounding students seem to be used to this scene, and the roll calling teacher's head doesn't even look up.

t_ch: 直到点名结束，你眼睁睁看着方才的土拨…皮皮西从你隔壁的座位底下钻出来，爬上椅子，长长地舒了口气。
t_en: Even till the roll call ends, you're still staring wide-eyed as the groundhog... Pepeshi appears from under a seat beside you, climbs into the chair, and lets out a long sigh.

t_ch: 呼…每次都跟打仗似的。果然，钱难赚、课难听，学长们诚不我欺…你说是这个理吧，同学？
t_en: Phew... It's like fighting a war every time. As expected, the money is hard, the lessons are perplexing, the seniors aren't wrong... you know?

t_ch: 原来不是行为艺术啊？
t_en: So it wasn't performance art?

t_ch: 嗨，行为艺术这玩意光烧钱不赚钱，咱是正经做生意的。「财富学院服务套餐」了解一下？
t_en: Bah, performance art is something that just costs you money. This is legitimate business. Care to learn more about the Fortune Academy Service Package?

t_ch: 帮人签到还能有钱赚？
t_en: You can make money by helping others take attendance?

t_ch: 呦，直击重点啊，一看你就是个懂行的。「财富学院服务套餐」了解一下？
t_en: Ha, straight to the point, I knew you were a sharp one. Care to learn more about the Fortune Academy Service Package?

t_ch: 你真是神通广大！
t_en: You're truly omnipotent!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，谬赞谬赞~商业机密不便透露，不过同学你要是实在感兴趣，「财富学院服务套餐」了解一下？
t_en: Heh-heh, flattering will not get you trade secrets, you know? But if you're truly interested, care to learn more about the Fortune Academy Service Package?

t_ch: 我这里不仅可以代人签到，还出售每节课的课堂笔记，甚至承接作业代写、学院代表签字服务，至于其它的嘛…只要价钱到位，也都好商量。
t_en: I don't only take attendance for others, I also sell lecture notes for each class. I even help do assignments, signature services for the academy's rep, and much more... Everything can be negotiated for the right price.

t_ch: 来来来，套餐种类多样，订购越多折扣越大，同学你要不要详细了解一下……
t_en: Come, come, I have plenty of package offers. The more you buy, the bigger the discounts. Care to learn more in detail...?

t_ch: 你在皮皮西热情的推销中度过了余下的小半堂课。随着铃声响起，你的心绪依旧如海潮般起伏不定，却早已不似当初。
t_en: The rest of the lesson goes by as you listen to the Pepeshi's enthused pitch. As the bell rings, your emotions are still wavering like the tide, but not as much as before.

t_ch: 唉，果然欢乐的时光总是短暂的，说起来…我有个不情之请不知该不该讲……
t_en: *sigh* Time flies when you're having fun. Speaking of which... I have a request that I'm not sure if I should say...

t_ch: 说呗，反正决定权在我。
t_en: Tell me, I'm the one deciding anyway.

t_ch: 那就别讲了。
t_en: Then you probably shouldn't.

t_ch: …是这样的，其实我对你一见如故，既然难得缘分一场当了回同桌，能不能跟我合个照，再签个名？
t_en: ...Actually, I felt an instant connection when I saw you. Since it's fate that we are desk mates, can we take a photograph together, then give me an autograph?

t_ch: 当然没问题！
t_en: Not a problem!

t_ch: 你与这位友好的皮皮西同学拍摄了合影，并慷慨地在照片后方签上了自己的名字。
t_en: You take a photo with your friendly Pepeshi classmate, and generously sign your name at the back of the photograph.

t_ch: 老板大气！呃…我是说非常感谢，我一定会好好收藏的！
t_en: Thank you, boss! Uh... I mean, I'm very grateful. I'll treasure it well!

t_ch: 还是算了吧…
t_en: I think we should forget it...

t_ch: 别这么谦虚嘛，相聚是缘，为了纪念我们的相遇，还是合个影吧！
t_en: Don't be so modest. It's fate that brought us together, so, to commemorate our meeting, let's take a photo together!

t_ch: 就这样，今天的第一堂课在讲师的「下堂课测试」以及学生们的唉声叹气中画上了圆满的句号——
t_en: Just like that, today's first lesson meets a satisfactory end with the lecturer's announcement of a class quiz for the next session along with the students' collective sighs.

t_ch: 什么，还不够圆满吗？你又不用考试！
t_en: What, not satisfactory enough? You don't even need to take the quiz!

t_ch: 我听财富学院的讲师说过了，你是来旁听的吧？课程马上就要开始了，快去找个位置坐好吧。
t_en: I heard the Fortune Academy lecturer say that you were just sitting in? The lesson is about to start. Hurry up and find a seat.

t_ch: 感谢提醒，我这就去。
t_en: Thanks for the reminder. I'm going now.

t_ch: 老师，我想去趟厕所！
t_en: I need to use the bathroom, teach!

t_ch: 俗话说得好，万事开头难。在经历过上一堂课的洗礼之后，你对接下来的课程充满信心。
t_en: As the saying goes, "the first step is always the most difficult." After enduring the previous lesson's trial by fire, you head into the next lesson with brimming confidence.

t_ch: 经济学需要一定的专业基础，听不懂情有可原。但画画总难不倒你吧？不就是照着……
t_en: Economics requires a certain level of basic understanding, so it's normal to not understand. But surely drawing's not a huge challenge for you? It's just copying...

t_ch: 好吧，是你僭越了。黑板上的图画你倒是姑且能看明白，但「定位轴线」是什么？「标高符号」是什么？「引出线」又是什么？
t_en: All right, you vastly overestimated your own abilities. You understand the drawing on the blackboard, but what's a "positioning axis"? Or an "elevation symbol"? And what's an "outlet line"?

t_ch: 你越是努力盯着黑板，那些线条便越是在你眼前模糊成一道道虚影——别问为什么人在梦中还会犯困，这大概就是知识的力量吧。
t_en: The harder you stare at the board, the more the lines start to blur before your eyes — don't ask why people still get tired in a dream. It's probably because of the power of knowledge.

t_ch: 你转头望向自己的邻座，想凭借一些亲切友好的交谈重归清醒，却只见到智械同学专注的侧脸。那人垂着头，笔尖在本子上「唰唰」扫出道道残影。
t_en: You turn to the seat beside you, hoping to return to consciousness with some friendly banter, only to see the side profile of your Intellitron classmate entrenched in deep concentration. This one has his head drooped, tracing streaks of afterimages with the intense swishing of his pen on his notebook.

t_ch: 你突然体会到一种近乎神圣的氛围——这人对于知识是如此虔诚、如此崇敬，以至于你想要冒昧搅扰的念头简直就是一种罪过。
t_en: You suddenly sense an almost-sacred atmosphere — this one is so devout, so respectful of knowledge, so much so that the mere notion of your offending disruption would be a sin.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}同学，你来复述一下，我刚刚讲到哪了？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, would you like to repeat where I just left off?

t_ch: 你问我，我问谁？
t_en: You're asking me, but who can I ask?

t_ch: …回答得很好，下节课别再来了。注意影响，课堂上别老是东张西望。
t_en: ...Good answer. You're dismissed for the next class. Watch your behavior, and stop looking around during class.

t_ch: 我知道，选C！
t_en: I know. It's C!

t_ch: 谁让你做选择题了？咳，注意影响，课堂上别老是东张西望。
t_en: Who said anything about multiple choice? Ahem, watch your behavior, and stop looking around during class.

t_ch: 算了，旁边那个智械小子，你起来答。
t_en: Forget it. That Intellitron fella beside you, you answer.

t_ch: 我选…C。
t_en: I choose ...C.

t_ch: 哼…一个两个，不听课就别来上课。难不成真以为只要全勤，期末成绩就能好看？
t_en: Ugh... You lot might as well not come to class if you won't listen. You really think your grades will look nicer just because you have full attendance?

t_ch: 你瞧瞧黑着脸不再搭理你们的讲师，又看看始终沉默着、努力减少自己存在感的邻座——这不应该啊？
t_en: You glance at your solemn-faced lecturer who is already ignoring you two, then at your silent neighbor who is trying his darnedest to reduce his presence — this shouldn't be happening?

t_ch: 你当然知道好奇害死猫，但你又不是猫；更何况，你还知道好奇心同样能憋死一个开拓者。于是你趁着所有人不备，悄悄探过头去——
t_en: You, of course, know that curiosity killed the cat, but you're not a cat. You also know that curiosity can also suffocate a Trailblazer. So you catch everyone off-guard and surreptitiously peek around...

t_ch: 这是…钟表小子？！
t_en: This is... Clockie!?

t_ch: 嘘！小、小点声，老师又看过来了……
t_en: Shhh! P—Pipe down! The teacher is looking over here again...

t_ch: 你在讲师恨铁不成钢的目光中瑟缩了一下，但随即就被眼前的画面吸引了全部注意——
t_en: You shrink under the lecturer's steely gaze, but are also wholly fascinated by the scene unfolding before you...

t_ch: 那流畅的线条、精美的画工、跃然纸上的角色…好家伙，原来这老兄还是个同人大佬！
t_en: Those smooth lines, exquisite penmanship, characters that leap off the page... Good golly, this person is a fanart master!

t_ch: …你…你喜欢…我的漫画？
t_en: ...You... You like... my comic?

t_ch: 你见猎心喜，压低嗓门表达了你对钟表小子原作以及眼前同人漫画的喜爱。沉默的智械听罢不再沉默，欢快地同你小声交流起来。
t_en: Delighted by what you see, you express your love for the original Clockie works and the artist's comic before you in a hushed voice. The silent Intellitron is no longer silent at your gushing, and happily converses with you in a similarly low whisper.

t_ch: 这节课在你们的窃窃私语中结束，充实而欢乐。你想，你或许已经爱上上课了。
t_en: This class ends amidst your whispering, a class that's both fulfilling and joyful. You think that maybe you've fallen in love with attending classes.

t_ch: 啊呀，你就是那个{NICKNAME}吧？很高兴能见到本人，希望你喜欢我的课哦~
t_en: Ah, you're that {NICKNAME}, right? Glad to meet you in person, and I hope you enjoy my class!

t_ch: 已经开始喜欢了！
t_en: I'm starting to like it!

t_ch: 我还想去趟厕所…
t_en: I still want to go to the bathroom...

t_ch: 如此亲切的讲师，授课的内容也想必不会太过困难吧——你对接下来的课程充满期待。
t_en: Such a warm and kind teacher. You are sure the lesson won't be too hard either — you enthusiastically look forward to the class.

t_ch: 你是谁？你可是大明星知更鸟的好朋友，列车留声机的忠实听众，作曲这点小事能难得倒你吗？
t_en: Who are you? You are a good friend of the superstar singer Robin's, a loyal listener of the Express' phonograph. Would composing a mere melody be any challenge to you?

t_ch: 好吧，还真能…说实在的，已经过去两节课了，你早就该学聪明了不是吗？不如看看你邻座的同学吧，说不定又是个可以相谈甚欢的……
t_en: All right, it really is a challenge... Honestly, two classes have come and gone — you should already have learned how to be smart, right? Why not take a gander at your neighbor instead? You might have another great conversation...

t_ch: 啾——啾啾？
t_en: Tweeeet, tweet tweet?

t_ch: 你说啥？我听不懂啊…
t_en: What did you say? I don't understand...

t_ch: 当然，作为那个「被选中的无名客」，你在折纸小鸟面前素来享有特权；但这并不影响你完全无法理解眼前的小鸟究竟想要表达些什么。
t_en: Of course, as that Chosen Nameless, you've always enjoyed perks before Origami Birds, but this does not hinder the fact that you completely don't understand what this bird is trying to communicate.

t_ch: 发出一声很酷的*哼*。
t_en: Let out a really cool sounding *hmph*.

t_ch: 在出声的那个瞬间，你清晰地看见隔壁桌上那只折纸小鸟的眼睛里出现了高光。
t_en: The moment it makes a sound, you clearly see a glint in the Origami Bird's eyes.

t_ch: 啾啾~啾——
t_en: Tweet tweet... Tweeeeeet.

t_ch: 你永远叫不醒一个装睡的人；同理，联觉信标永远翻译不了一句纯粹的「啾」。但现在放弃还为时尚早，或许你可以找个翻译——
t_en: You can never awaken someone who's pretending to be sleeping. Similarly, the Synesthesia Beacon will never be able to translate a bright "tweet." But it's still too early to give up. Perhaps you can find a translation...

t_ch: 你抬头四顾，然而偌大教室中除了你和那位讲师，竟没有一个正常的…「人」。
t_en: You look around, but apart from you and that lecturer, no one else around the classroom is a normal... person.

t_ch: 罢了，你累了，或许人与人之间就是永远也无法相互理解，或许这课堂之上本就无你一方容身之所……
t_en: Forget it. You're tired. Maybe people will never be able to understand each other. Maybe you never belonged in this class in the first place...

t_ch: 一个纸团「啪——」地落在你的桌面上。
t_en: A paper ball falls on your desk with a soft thud.

t_ch: 哦，你知道这个，课上悄悄话的必备项目！你的课堂体验愈发圆满了——
t_en: Oh, you know this move — an essential tool for clandestine comms in class! Your classroom experience is becoming much more fruitful now...

t_ch: 对啊，在梦中不就该无拘无束自由自在吗？原来格格不入的并非真正的你，而是可恶的惯性思维，你决定加入你的同学——
t_en: That's right. Shouldn't one be carefree and unfettered in a dream? You're not the one who is incapable of fitting in. It's those blasted habitual thoughts of yours. You decide to join your classmate...

t_ch: 扮演成钟表小子。
t_en: Roleplay as Clockie.

t_ch: 嘿~滴答！我的朋友，钟表小子来了！
t_en: Heyyy tick-tock! My friend, Clockie is here!

t_ch: 啾！
t_en: Tweet!

t_ch: 你的表演非常还原，{NICKNAME}同学。但请小点声，不要影响大家听课哦~
t_en: Your performance is impeccable, {NICKNAME}. But please keep it down and not disrupt the class for others.

t_ch: 扮演成哈努兄弟。
t_en: Roleplay as Brother Hanu.

t_ch: 哼。
t_en: Hmph.

t_ch: 啾啾~
t_en: Tweet tweet!

t_ch: 只是变换了一个形态，你们之间的壁垒便轰然瓦解——此刻你只觉与眼前的「折纸小鸟」心有灵犀、不点就通！
t_en: No sooner than you change your demeanor, the walls between you crumble — at this moment, your thoughts are one with the Origami Bird before you, absolutely in sync!

t_ch: 扮演成折纸小鸟。
t_en: Roleplay as an Origami Bird.

t_ch: 啾啾！
t_en: Tweet tweet!

t_ch: 啾啾——啾！
t_en: Tweet tweeeet, tweet!

t_ch: 似有看不见的线将你们牵引至一处，你只觉眼前的「折纸小鸟」让你产生了家人般的亲切感。
t_en: It's as if an invisible thread connects the both of you to a single place, as you witness the origami bird form a kinship with you only reserved for familial ties.

t_ch: 果然，人与人之间终究还是能够相互理解，而这课堂之上总归也还有你一方容身之所！
t_en: Indeed, people can still understand one another eventually, and this class still has a place for you!

t_ch: 就这样，在一派和乐融融的气氛中，你的课堂体验圆满落幕——
t_en: Just like that, amidst a united, harmonious atmosphere, your classroom experience comes to a satisfactory close...

t_ch: 至于自始至终都没听懂的课程？说真的，除了筑梦学院那位讲师之外，谁在乎呢。
t_en: As for the class that you couldn't understand from start to end? Honestly, apart from that Dreamweaver Academy lecturer, who cares about those lessons?

t_ch: 请仔细核对课表…
t_en: Please look through the class schedule carefully...

t_ch: 就要上课了，快坐好！
t_en: Class is about to begin. Take your seats, everybody!

t_ch: 哼~哼哼~
t_en: Hm~ Hm hm...

t_ch: 在这位同学的软磨硬泡下，你最终还是拍摄了合影，并在照片后方签上了名字。
t_en: Thanks to the student's constant pestering, in the end, you agree to take a photo together and sign on the back of it.

t_ch: 不就是卖个签名照嘛…
t_en: It's just selling an autographed photo...

t_ch: 不就是拍卖了盛会巨星的签名照嘛，至于闹到学校…噫，怎么你也在？！
t_en: It's just auctioning off an autographed photo of the Festive Superstar. As for the trouble at school... Huh? Why are you here as well!?

t_ch: 先说清楚，我没有卖出去啊…所以没从中盈利！
t_en: Let me make one thing clear: I didn't sell it to anybody, so I didn't profit from it anyway!

t_ch: 这是未遂！
t_en: You still made an attempt!

t_ch: 没有可以分给我的钱吗？
t_en: So, I don't get a cut?

t_ch: 哎，才挂到论坛上没多久呢，就有个自称是什么忍徒的家伙把我给举报了，帖子被删了，我也被喊来校长室，挨了好久的批评……
t_en: Well, not long after I posted the listing to the forum, somebody claiming to be a ninja initiate blew the whistle on me, causing the post to be deleted. I was also summoned to the dean's office and given a stern talking-to...

t_ch: 我说，那个忍徒该不会就是你吧？
t_en: Speaking of which, that ninja initiate... wasn't you, right?

t_ch: 不是，但我也要举报你。
t_en: No, but I do want to report you as well.

t_ch: 这是正义的惩罚！
t_en: These are your just deserts!

t_ch: 我不是忍徒，而是忍侠！
t_en: I'm not a ninja initiate. I'm a Ninja Hero!

t_ch: 行吧，反正已经被学校发现，是谁都无所谓了。时运不济，发财的路多灾多难啊。
t_en: Fine, since the school already discovered this, the ninja initiate's identity doesn't matter anymore. When times are bad, the road to wealth is full of potholes.

t_ch: 尽管不知道那位学生是谁，但你还是要在心底默默地说——谢谢你，无名的忍徒！
t_en: Even though you still don't know who that student is, you can't help but thank them silently from the bottom of your heart: Thank you, anonymous ninja initiate!

t_ch: 哎，这可怎么办是好…都这个时间了，再拖下去就赶不上校庆了。
t_en: *sigh* What'll we do... It's already this late, and we won't make the school anniversary if this keeps up.

t_ch: 你好像遇到了麻烦。
t_en: You seem to have run into trouble.

t_ch: 是啊。我是电影放映部的成员，原本打算要在校庆上播放社团拍摄的作品来着，可部长…啊，就是预定的导演，临时退出了，目前的进度很不妙。
t_en: We have. I'm from the Film Club — we intended to play a film the club made at the anniversary, but our director... Ah, I mean the appointed director, he suddenly quit, so it's not looking good right now.

t_ch: 咦…你不是传说中的盛会巨星吗！我在论坛上看过好多关于你的帖子，听说你超有才的！有空的话，来帮帮我们吧！
t_en: Hey... Aren't you the legendary Festive Superstar!? I've seen so many posts of you on the forums. I heard you're really talented! Please help us if you've got time!

t_ch: 你们的部长为什么突然退出…
t_en: Why did your director suddenly quit...

t_ch: 我不太清楚，可能嫌弃我们只是个学生社团吧，没什么经费，设备和拍摄技术也很差，说是要把电影作为人生志向，却总拍些无聊透顶的片子。
t_en: I'm not too sure. Maybe he felt we were just a small new student society with no future. Our budget's meager, our equipment and filming skills are pretty bad, and for all that talk about filmmaking being our life's aspiration, we only film dumb student shorts.

t_ch: 部长在芮克先生的摄影棚实习过，算是见过大场面了，瞧不起这里也正常。
t_en: The director used to intern under Mr. Reca, so he's probably seen what real filming is like. It's only normal that he's scoffing at our efforts.

t_ch: 拍电影都要做些什么？
t_en: What do I need to do to film a movie?

t_ch: 很简单的，我教你！看到旁边的拼图了吗，首先，我们把场景布置好……
t_en: It's really simple. I'll teach you! See that jigsaw by the side? First, we lay out the scene...

t_ch: 对不起，我很忙的。
t_en: Sorry, I'm super busy.

t_ch: 好吧…我再想想办法。
t_en: All right... I'll think of something else.

t_ch: 请先完成拼图，布置好电影场景。准备就绪后，我们进行后面的拍摄步骤。
t_en: Finish the jigsaw first and lay out the scene. When you're ready, we'll go through the subsequent filming process.

t_ch: 好了！这就是我们的电影场景，虽然简单了点，但就这次拍摄而言也够用了。
t_en: Awright! This is our film set. It's a little plain, but enough for this filming session.

t_ch: 都到偷工减料的程度了。
t_en: You're cutting a few too many corners...

t_ch: 经费真就这么少？
t_en: The budget is really this tight?

t_ch: 评判电影好坏的标准不在于成本，而是质量。你瞧瞧克劳克影城那么大的面积，不也没拍出几部传世佳作嘛。没事的，要相信导演的能力。
t_en: The standard for film evaluation is in its quality, not its budget. Take a look at how big Clock Studios is — they haven't really made any major hits, have they? Don't worry. Trust in the director's eye.

t_ch: 接下来，就请通过海选的演员入场——
t_en: Next, we'll have the actors who passed the screen test enter...

t_ch: 一阵清脆的碰撞声，出现在你面前的是……
t_en: A crisp bang, and the one appearing before you is...

t_ch: 剧组的各位，大家好。我叫阿红，从小就作为童星在电影中担任过主要角色，拥有极其丰富的演艺经验，特技是「五秒内就能喷出来」。请多指教。
t_en: Hello to everyone in the production crew. My name is Reddie. I've played main characters in movies as a child star, and I have ample acting experience. My special is "spitting it out in five seconds." I'll be in your care.

t_ch: 我、我叫小金…没接触过演艺圈，这是第一次参加电影拍摄。呃…谢谢。
t_en: I—I'm Goldie... I've never professionally acted, so this is the first time I'm participating in a film. Umm... Thank you.

t_ch: …绿。按剧本来就好是吧？我会完成我的工作，就这些。
t_en: ...Greenie. Just follow the script, yeah? I'll get my work done. That's all from me.

t_ch: 各位演员的特色都相当明显啊，在开拍前，导演有什么想说的吗？
t_en: Each actor's got their own unique traits. Is there anything the director would like to say before we start shooting?

t_ch: 特色确实挺明显的…
t_en: Their traits sure are unique indeed...

t_ch: 哈哈，现在的电影讲究的就是人设突出、标签清晰！这下可不会有观众来抱怨说分不清角色谁是谁了——他该去看医生，而不是看电影！
t_en: Haha, movies nowadays are all about obvious character traits and clear stereotypes! The audience won't be griping about not being able to differentiate between characters this time — such people should be seeing a doctor, not a movie!

t_ch: 都是老演员了，我相信它们。
t_en: They're all veterans, and I trust them.

t_ch: 听起来导演和这些酒瓶很熟？那就好，瞧瞧它们饱满的姿态，即将满溢而出的热情——这就是老艺术家的从容！
t_en: Sounds like the director's very familiar with these drink bottles? That's good then. Look at their brimming poise, almost bursting with passion — that's the swagger of a seasoned artiste!

t_ch: 我要和演员们聊聊
t_en: I want to chat with the actors.

t_ch: 当然，这是导演的权利，了解演员的性格才能更好地把控剧本走向——直接向它们问话吧。
t_en: Of course, that's only your right, to get to know the actors to better commandeer the set direction. You can speak to them directly.

t_ch: 询问红色酒瓶。
t_en: Talk to the red bottle.

t_ch: 阿红。我想知道，你为什么要进入演艺圈？
t_en: Reddie, I'd like to know why you joined show business.

t_ch: 我的话，应该是受过去经历的影响吧。我很早就接触了演员这个职业，并取得了还不错的成就，所以就沿着这条路走下来了。
t_en: It's probably because I was influenced by my past experiences. I was exposed to this industry from an early age and attained decent success, so I just decided to stick with it.

t_ch: 算不上有多喜欢，但绝对不讨厌。并且，这是我的工作，需要的不是无谓的热情，而是足够的能力和认真的态度。
t_en: I can't say I really love it, but it's not something that irks me. And it's my job — what's required isn't meaningless passion, but sufficient ability and a serious attitude.

t_ch: （这位演员相当专业，目前没发现什么问题。）
t_en: (This actor's pretty professional. I haven't noticed any problems yet.)

t_ch: 询问金色酒瓶。
t_en: Talk to the gold bottle.

t_ch: 小金。我想知道，你为什么要进入演艺圈？
t_en: Goldie, I'd like to know why you joined show business.

t_ch: 咦，在问我吗？可能是…我想成为所有人目光的中心，在大家面前闪闪发光！这是我始终的梦想，也朝这个目标一直努力到了现在。
t_en: Huh, you asking me? It's probably because... I want to be in the limelight of everyone's eyes, to shine bright in front of them! This has always been my dream, and I've been working hard to achieve this goal.

t_ch: 绝对要成为全梦境最著名的演员！加油啊，小金！
t_en: I must become the most famous actor in all of the Dreamscape! I can do it!

t_ch: （这位演员对演艺事业相当有激情，但能力或许还有所欠缺。）
t_en: (This actor is pretty enthusiastic about acting, but its skill might be left wanting.)

t_ch: 询问绿色酒瓶。
t_en: Talk to the green bottle.

t_ch: 绿。我想知道，你为什么想进入演艺圈？
t_en: Greenie, I'd like to know why you joined show business.

t_ch: 别问，问就是我有这个天赋，也有这个需要。仅此而已。
t_en: Don't ask me. My answer is that I have the talent, and there is a need for it. That's all.

t_ch: （这位演员性格有些孤高，不好沟通，但或许是个实力派。）
t_en: (This actor's character is quite aloof, and is difficult to communicate with. But maybe it's the real deal.)

t_ch: 事不宜迟，我们开拍吧。
t_en: Without further ado, let's start the shoot.

t_ch: 收到！请各位演员站到指定位置，我们的拍摄马上就要开始了。
t_en: Got it! Actors at your designated spots please. We're going to start rolling.

t_ch: 各位演员都熟读过设定了，我们就直接开始。我来担任旁白，导演注意把控情节的走向，在必要的时候做出决定。
t_en: Now that the actors are familiar with their settings, we'll start right now. I'll be in charge of the narration. The director will control the plot's direction, and make decisions when the need arises.

t_ch: 时间差不多咯，准备好——摄影机，启动！
t_en: It's about time. Everyone ready... Cameras, action!

t_ch: 即将落幕的校庆，所有学生都在为成为舞台上的明星而奋力向前，其中，三名选手的身影尤为引人注目。它们分别是——
t_en: The school anniversary is soon to end, and all the students are clamoring forth to become stars on the stage. Among them, three candidates stand out brilliantly — they are...

t_ch: 看啊，那高贵的红毯正符合我的色彩，那璀璨的聚光灯正为了衬托我的光泽而亮起，那人群和呐喊声正在为我欢呼——我即是那最闪耀的星。
t_en: Look, that elegant red carpet matches my color perfectly. The glitzy lights bring out my hue and make it dazzle even shinier. The crowd's screams are their tribute to me — I am the brightest star around here.

t_ch: 不会让给你的！我才是…才是最适合站在舞台中心的酒瓶，没有谁能阻碍我！
t_en: I won't let you have it! I deserve it the most... The bottle most qualified to stand on stage is me! No one else will get in my way!

t_ch: 可笑。带着你的白日梦滚回酒窖去吧。我会击败在这的所有酒瓶，登上明星巅峰。
t_en: Pathetic. You should slink back with your daydreams. I will defeat all the bottles here and ascend the stage of stardom.

t_ch: 何等激烈的竞争！三位选手明显都是有备而来，对舞台的主角位置志在必得！那么，经过紧张刺激的比拼，谁将成为最终的获胜者呢？
t_en: What an intense bout! These three contenders have obviously come prepared, all for the singular goal of hogging center stage! Now then, with this thrilling clash unfolding, who will become the eventual victor?

t_ch: 最终会是谁呢…会是谁呢…是谁呢…是谁……
t_en: Who will it be... Will it be... Will it... be...

t_ch: 导演，快决定一下啊！
t_en: Director! Hurry up and make a decision!

t_ch: 啊？剧本不是写好了吗？
t_en: Eh? I thought the script already decided it?

t_ch: 这个由我说了算？
t_en: Oh, I can call the shots?

t_ch: 我们的剧本都是在拍摄过程中完成的，讲究的就是随机应变、敏捷开发，这样才能更好地激发出演员和剧组的创意，避免落入俗套的情节模式。
t_en: All our scripts are finished during filming. Our emphasis is on spontaneity and agile development, and that's how we can better evoke creativity from the actors and crew, to avoid the pitfalls of cliched plots.

t_ch: 按照导演对各位演员的理解，给出最后的结局吧，中间部分会由我来补全的！
t_en: From what you know of them, you should announce the final outcome. I'll fill up everything else in the middle!

t_ch: （根据演员的性格和能力来安排剧情…这样临时改动真的没问题么？）
t_en: (Arranging the script according to the actors' personalities and abilities... Will such spur-of-the-moment changes really not present problems?)

t_ch: 回忆对三位演员的评价。
t_en: Recall your evaluation of the three actors.

t_ch: （「阿红」相当专业，目前没发现什么问题。）
t_en: (Reddie's pretty professional. I haven't noticed any problems yet.)

t_ch: （「小金」对演艺事业相当有激情，但能力或许还有所欠缺。）
t_en: (Goldie is pretty enthusiastic about acting, but its skill might be left wanting.)

t_ch: （「绿」的性格有些孤高，不好沟通，但或许是个实力派。）
t_en: (Greenie's character is quite aloof, and is difficult to communicate with. But maybe it's the real deal.)

t_ch: 我想好了。
t_en: I'm ready.

t_ch: 导演已经决定了，就由……
t_en: The director has decided, and it'll be...

t_ch: 红色酒瓶来担任主角。
t_en: The red bottle will play the protagonist.

t_ch: 毫无疑问，阿红是所有选手中综合实力最为强劲的，按常规来看，她理应成为舞台的焦点。剧情会不会如所有人预料的这般发展呢……
t_en: Without a doubt, Reddie is the most capable among all the candidates. For all intents and purposes, she should be the highlight of the stage. Will any unforeseen twists occur in the script though...?

t_ch: 金色酒瓶来担任主角。
t_en: The gold bottle will play the protagonist.

t_ch: 相当有魄力的选择！尽管小金选手满怀对舞台的憧憬，可她的演艺经验尚浅，这份热情能否支撑她击败所有对手，走到聚光灯的中心呢……
t_en: A choice very bold! Even with Goldie's unbridled passion for the stage, her acting experience remains lacking. Will this enthusiasm prop her up in defeating her competitors, and guide her toward the light of the center stage...?

t_ch: 绿色酒瓶来担任主角。
t_en: The green bottle will play the protagonist.

t_ch: 故事最重要的就是悬念，不是吗？我们对绿选手几乎一无所知，正因如此，我们才相信他能为舞台带来意料外的变化。他是否能成为那最大的变数……
t_en: Suspense is imperative in a story, is it not? We know almost nothing about Greenie, and that's why we believe he will bring about the most unanticipated change on the stage. Will he turn out to be the biggest variable...?

t_ch: 让我们拭目以待吧——
t_en: Let us stay tuned!

t_ch: 阿红选手以压倒性的优势击败了所有对手，是的，毫无悬念，这就是绝对的实力碾压！
t_en: Reddie has defeated all opponents with its overwhelming advantage in talent. Yes, indubitably, this is what we call "not even a fair fight"!

t_ch: 我们的故事到这里就要结束了吗，观众们能接受如此平铺直叙的剧情吗？票房撑得住吗？
t_en: Will our story call it a day here? Will the audience accept such an obvious plot? Will ticket sales be able to hang in there?

t_ch: 别急，还有反转。
t_en: Don't worry, plot twist incoming.

t_ch: 噢！还有反转！会是怎样惊人的展开呢？所有人都等着，只见阿红选手迅捷地跳起……
t_en: Oh! A twist! What jaw-dropping display will there be? Everyone is waiting, to see Reddie dexterously jump...

t_ch: 然后稳稳地倒立在了舞台上！
t_en: And then do a convincing handstand on the stage!

t_ch: 全场爆发出雷鸣般的掌声，普通的艺人遵守规则，天生的明星引领潮流！在铺天盖地的欢呼声中，我们的故事终于迎来了尾声，符合预期却又始料未及的结局！
t_en: The whole room erupts with thundering applause — Ordinary artistes stay in their lane, while it is the gifted stars who will blaze the trail! Amidst raucous cheers and clapping, our story has finally come to an end — a most expected yet surprising ending!

t_ch: ——全剧终。
t_en: ...The end.

t_ch: 咳、咳咳…咳咳…我的头很难受……
t_en: *cough* *cough cough* ...My head hurts...

t_ch: 拍摄结束了，感觉还不错吧？哎呀，不愧是著名童星，临场表现能这么出色。
t_en: The shoot's over. Not bad, eh? Gosh, that was absolutely the acting chops of a child star. Such an outstanding spontaneous performance.

t_ch: 但你听见了红色酒瓶发出的连续不断的咳嗽声，或许是倒立这个动作对香槟来说实在过于困难——你敢肯定它现在绝对憋了一肚子的气。
t_en: But you hear the red bottle's non-stop coughing — perhaps the handstand was too much for a champagne bottle. You're sure it's definitely feeling bloated.

t_ch: 太敬业了，愿意为了电影效果而牺牲健康，你就是天生的演员！下次拍摄还找你！
t_en: Such dedication to your craft, to sacrifice your own health for a better scene. You are a born actor! I must have you the next time we shoot!

t_ch: 你不确定这种行为是否值得提倡，但至少，你绝对不希望它发生在自己身上。
t_en: You're not sure if such behavior is worth lauding, but at the very least, you hope it never has to happen to you.

t_ch: ——你对舞台的强烈渴望，正是成为主角的理由。
t_en: ...Your unquenchable thirst for the stage is the reason you're protagonist material.

t_ch: 初次对战，小金选手便迅速败下阵来。论外表的光鲜色泽，她不如阿红；论内在的沉稳气质，她也比不上绿。那她该用什么来证明自己的独特魅力？
t_en: In the first match-up, Goldie is quickly ejected off-stage. She pales in comparison to Reddie in hue and color, and she lacks the gravitas that Greenie can pull off. How then, can she flaunt her own unique charm?

t_ch: 小金选手思考许久，终于得出了结论：她拥有的，只有从内心深处*迸发*出的热情。
t_en: Goldie thinks for a while, finally coming to her own conclusion: What she has, is a surging passion that *sprays* forth from deep in the crevices of her desire.

t_ch: 是的。在最终对决的舞台上，小金爆发出了惊人的气势——她喷出的气泡，足足掀翻了舞台上方的巨大气球！谁能想到她其实是一瓶红酒呢？
t_en: Yes. In the final battle on stage, Goldie explodes with shocking poise — she spits out enough bubbles to topple a giant balloon above the stage! Who would even have thought that she is actually just a bottle of red wine?

t_ch: 我抓住了那颗星星，却摔得粉碎……
t_en: I caught that star, but it fell to pieces...

t_ch: 为了制造出最漂亮的喷泉，小金猛烈摇晃自己的身体，直至玻璃壁都四分五裂。但观众们都相信，那将会是他们此生见过的…最壮丽的景象。
t_en: To create the most beautiful fountain, Goldie violently shakes her own body, to the point where her glass walls start to crack. But the audience wholeheartedly believes that it's probably... the most magnificent sight they have ever witnessed in their lives.

t_ch: ——全剧终。
t_en: ...The end.

t_ch: 拍摄结束了，感觉还不错吧？哎呀，没想到新人演员的表现能这么出色。
t_en: The shoot's over. Not bad, eh? Never thought a newcomer's performance would be so outstanding.

t_ch: 但你看见金色的酒瓶躺倒在桌面上——它的瓶身已经变得破碎，其中的液体正缓慢渗透出来。很明显，它已经没法再回应你的话语了。
t_en: But you see the golden bottle lying on the desk — it has been shattered, its liquid slowly seeping out. It's clear that it can no longer reply to your words.

t_ch: 演员为了诠释角色，将自己的生命燃烧殆尽…很符合电影剧情，我喜欢这个结局。
t_en: To fully interpret her character, the actor burnt her life to cinders... A fitting movie plot, I like this ending.

t_ch: 你不清楚金色酒瓶的真实想法，对它而言这样的结果算是幸福吗？
t_en: You're unsure of the gold bottle's true thoughts. Is this outcome really worth it for the bottle?

t_ch: 能确定的是，在折纸大学杂乱的房间里——一个酒瓶的演员梦，轻轻地碎了。
t_en: What is certain, is that in a cluttered room in Paperfold University, a bottle's acting dream has been softly smashed.

t_ch: 绿选手，完全的谜。我们仍未知道他的具体信息，他就像是瓶身上那抹绿色般深沉而捉摸不透。但没关系，面对这场激烈的较量，他必须拿出全力才行！
t_en: Greenie, a complete enigma. We still don't know anything about him. His character is like that green body of his — ambiguous and unfathomable. But it's fine, for in this intense battle of acting, he'll have to pull out all the stops!
